title: salix × bifida subsp. pseudoelaeagnos
intro: Salix × bifida subsp. pseudoelaeagnos, also known as the pseudo-Elm or Bifid Willow, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between two different willow species. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to help reduce fever and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Salix × bifida subsp. pseudoelaeagnos include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as arthritis, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
